title: Verifier v1 result schema (Research Governance v0.1, PR 15 + Autoresearch MVP-0.1.1)
description: Output of `scripts/verify_candidate.sh --json <path>`. Records the deterministic verdict of one verification run. MVP-0.1.1 (Critic state hardening) extends the schema with three new fields (critic_report_is_template, critic_completed, critic_status) and bumps schema_version to 1.2.

candidate_id.description: Identifier from <candidate-dir>/manifest.toml::[candidate].id; falls back to the basename of <candidate-dir> when running without --candidate.

candidate_dir.description: Path of the candidate directory, or null when the verifier was invoked tree-level (no --candidate).

reasons.description: Empty for PASS_SHAPE_ONLY. One entry per failed/HRR check otherwise.

checks.description: Per-check sub-statuses. Keys are the check names; values are PASS/FAIL/HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED/SKIPPED/UNKNOWN. Absence of a key means the check did not run.

axioms_allowed.description: Allowed axioms for the closed final term, mirrored from spec/target.toml::[axioms].allowed at run time.

spec_version.description: Value of spec/target.toml::[meta].spec_version at run time.

critic_report_present.description: MVP-4: whether <candidate>/critic_report.md exists on disk. The Verifier does NOT require this file (the Critic runs after the Verifier per spec/critic_protocol.md §4); the field is reported for downstream tooling that decides whether to record critic_status = not_run vs. parse the report.

critic_report_is_template.description: MVP-0.1.1: whether the parsed critic_report.md still carries template markers (the <!-- TEMPLATE_MARKER --> HTML comment, or the literal 'Template placeholder' text in every attack section). Computed by scripts/validate_critic_report.py. False when critic_report_present is false.

critic_completed.description: MVP-0.1.1: critic_report_present && !critic_report_is_template && all six attack sections present and well-formed && Verdict block has critic_status in {pass, fail}. A 'completed' Critic report is the only kind that scripts/validate_jsonl.py::validate_attempt accepts as evidence for critic_status = pass / fail in outputs/attempts.jsonl.

critic_status.description: MVP-0.1.1: the Verifier-side view of Critic status. Per spec/critic_protocol.md §4 the Critic runs after the Verifier, so this is always 'not_run' in result.json regardless of whether a (potentially filled) critic_report.md is present. Real pass/fail values appear only in the Critic's outputs/attempts.jsonl line.

completed_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at end of the run. Optional; verify_candidate.sh emits it for human inspection but it is NOT part of the canonical hash for Rule 13 reproducibility.
